message:
fix(storage): wrap v0 init triple in BEGIN IMMEDIATE/COMMIT for atomic rollback (ft-k542h)

The existing-v0-database init path ran repair_existing_v0_tables_before_schema_sql
+ SCHEMA_SQL + run_migrations(0) + ensure_ft_meta sequentially without a
transaction wrapper (c06b230a fresh-eyes review finding). A crash between
repair and run_migrations could leave half-applied ALTERs with user_version
unchanged, and the next open would re-trigger repair on already-mutated tables.

Fix: extract the v0 init body into run_v0_init_in_transaction. The helper
applies SCHEMA_SQL's PRAGMA preamble outside any transaction (SQLite rejects
journal_mode/synchronous changes inside a tx) and runs the table/index body
+ repair + run_migrations + ensure_ft_meta inside one BEGIN IMMEDIATE / COMMIT.
ROLLBACK on any error.

Also makes apply_migration_step skip its own per-step BEGIN/COMMIT when
conn.is_autocommit() is false (i.e., when called from inside the new outer
transaction). Outer caller's ROLLBACK already provides atomicity for the
wrapped scope; nested BEGIN would otherwise be rejected by SQLite.

Test: storage::tests::v0_init_fault_after_repair_rolls_back_atomically.
Bootstraps the canonical schema, regresses audit_actions to a legacy v0
shape (drops correlation_id + its index, resets user_version=0), injects a
fault between repair and SCHEMA_SQL via cfg(test) V0_INIT_FAULT_AT, asserts
that:
  - the fault propagates as a MigrationFailed error
  - user_version is still 0 (no migration committed)
  - repair's ALTER did not survive (correlation_id absent)
  - connection is back in autocommit mode (no leaked BEGIN)
  - re-running initialize_schema completes successfully

Closes ft-k542h.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
